compiled_state.title: debug-everything session cleanup (lock release + epic notes) lost after context compaction
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description: After context compaction during an extensive debug-everything session, the Phase 9 cleanup instructions (lock-release, epic closure/summary notes, discovery cleanup) are lost from context. This leaves stale session locks (requiring LOCK_STALE reclaim on next run) and stale Project Health Restoration epics without proper summary notes. Root cause: Phase 9/10/11 instructions are far from the active working context and get compacted away during long sessions. The graceful shutdown path (Phase 9 'On Graceful Shutdown') should be triggered by compaction events, but the compaction event itself causes the instructions to be unavailable. Potential fixes: (1) Add a compaction-resilient cleanup hook that runs lock-release and epic summary independently of skill context, (2) Move critical cleanup commands into a standalone script that can be invoked with minimal context, (3) Add lock TTL auto-expiry so stale locks self-heal without requiring LOCK_STALE reclaim.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Ticket references orchestrator behavior (cleanup lost after compaction) AND affected file is skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md. Dual-signal detected.
comments.author: Main

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed

comments.body: Bot-psychologist: LOCK_ID not persisted to artifact file, lost on compaction. Fix: persist to artifact file + add CONTEXT ANCHOR with recovery instruction.
comments.author: Main
